events.text: {"decision":"fail","risk":"high","work_stage":"rough-complete","component_conditions":["installed-defective","non-compliant"],"percent_complete":80,"value":0,"sound_source":"","confidence":0.9,"event_time":0,"rate":0,"order":[],"workable":[],"findings":["DP impulse lines sloped incorrectly for service: gas taps below pipe / liquid taps on top, causing condensate/liquid accumulation in gas service or gas trapping in liquid service, leading to erroneous measurement","Impulse tubing arrangement does not meet API RP 551 slope requirements for the applicable service"],"actions":["Reconfigure impulse tubing to correct slope per service: for gas service, slope tubing upward from tap to transmitter so condensate drains back to process; for liquid service, slope downward and avoid high points that trap gas","Verify against engineered drawings and manufacturer installation requirements, then re-inspect before closeout"],"rationale":"API RP 551 requires impulse piping/tubing to be sloped to prevent accumulation of condensate or gas in the wrong service. Gas service taps below the pipe and liquid taps on top are the classic misarrangement that will trap fluids/gases and cause measurement error or damage. Work is roughed in but non-compliant and must be reworked before closeout.","references":["API RP 551","Manufacturer installation manuals"]}
